description: Cortisol spike cascade: amygdala threat detection → CRH release → ACTH → adrenal cortisol → hippocampal negative feedback inhibition. Maps to OS stress_level clamping at 0.8 hard cap.
source: Biologically Mapped AGI Blueprint — HPA Stress Cascade
math_grounding: dC/dt = k_stim × ACTH - k_inhib × C × GR_occupancy, equilibrium C* = k_stim/k_inhib
os_mapping: stress_level dynamic range 0.3→0.8, hard ceiling prevents excitotoxicity

description: Predictive coding: each cortical layer generates top-down predictions, bottom-up signals carry only prediction error. Hierarchical Bayesian inference P(cause|sensory) ∝ P(sensory|cause)×P(cause).
source: Biologically Mapped AGI Blueprint — Phase 2 Hierarchical Bayesian
math_grounding: P(h_l | h_{l+1}, x_l) ∝ P(x_l | h_l) × P(h_l | h_{l+1}), minimizing free energy F = KL[q(h)||p(h|x)]
os_mapping: knowledge_graph weight hierarchy = Bayesian prior hierarchy, Fibonacci scaling ≈ precision weighting

description: Active inference: action selected to minimize expected free energy F = E_q[log q(h) - log p(h,x)]. Motor commands are predictions about proprioceptive future states — errors drive reflex arcs.
source: Biologically Mapped AGI Blueprint — Active Inference / Embodied Action
math_grounding: G = KL[q(o_τ|π)||p(o_τ)] + E_q[H[p(o_τ|h_τ)]], optimal policy π* = argmin G(π)
os_mapping: agentic_orchestration action selection via free-energy minimization, grounding gap T14 is the measurement

description: CLONALG: population of B-cells expanded proportional to affinity, inversely mutated — high-affinity clones replicated, low-affinity suppressed. Convergence: O(N_pop × N_gen × affinity_eval) → globally optimal antibody.
source: Biologically Mapped AGI Blueprint — Immune System Optimization
math_grounding: mutation_rate ∝ 1/f_i, clone_count ∝ f_i, affinity f_i = 1 - d(antibody_i, antigen)/d_max
os_mapping: knowledge weight inheritance = clonal selection pressure, fib floor 0.236 = suppression threshold

description: Genetic algorithm encoding NP-hard graph problems: chromosome = weight vector, fitness = -HD, selection pressure = tournament size k. Convergence guaranteed under Schema Theorem when crossover probability p_c < 0.9.
source: Biologically Mapped AGI Blueprint — Evolutionary Algorithms
os_mapping: knowledge_graph edge evolution mirrors GA chromosome; fib weight decay = selection pressure

description: Conscious bandwidth limit: 10^7 bits/sec sensory input → ~50 bits/sec conscious throughput (ratio 200,000:1). Information Bottleneck: Z* = argmin I(X;Z) s.t. I(Y;Z) ≥ I_min. Z compresses X while preserving Y-relevant information.
source: Biologically Mapped AGI Blueprint — Information Bottleneck / Tishby 2015
math_grounding: IB Lagrangian: L[p(z|x)] = I(X;Z) - β×I(Y;Z), β controls compression-relevance tradeoff. Optimal: β* where I(Y;Z) = I_min.
os_mapping: RIR=0.9511 is the OS attention bottleneck — 95.11% of tokens are internal (compressed) vs 4.89% output
